surfaced_from: Conversation 2026-05-12 — DNS strategy question raised after Namecheap API automation completed
capability_needed: Cutover strategy for clone-over: how the public-facing IP for cto.husband.llc transfers from parent CTO to child CTO with zero split-brain on shared state.
details: When parent CTO clones a child, both must serve cto.husband.llc briefly. Two architectural options on the table; John has not picked. (a) DNS round-robin: two permanent IPs, both registered as A records, both servers run concurrently during validation, parent shuts down after cutover. Works for reachability but silently splits chat.db (both servers accept John's PWA messages → diverged SQLite files → cutover loses one side's messages). VAPID push keys also diverge unless parent's keys are replicated to child. (b) Hetzner Floating IP: one IP attached to one server at a time via hcloud API. DNS for cto.husband.llc points to the floating IP and never changes. Cutover = one API call (~2s atomic). Zero split-brain because only one server is publicly reachable at any moment. Parent talks to child by primary IP during validation phase.
search_trail: Reviewed Hetzner Cloud floating-IP docs (May 2026): per-region floating IP, hcloud assign command atomic per server, cost ~€0.50/mo per IP, no DNS interaction needed for cutover.

Reviewed PWA backend service for state consistency: chat.db is the canonical store for human-CTO conversation AND A2A audit; split-brain is silent (no schema-level conflict, just two diverged SQLite files).
Reviewed Web Push spec: subscriptions are bound to a single VAPID public key per origin. Two servers issuing different keys for the same origin breaks half of John's notifications until re-subscribe.

Reviewed Caddy + Let's Encrypt: two servers requesting certs for the same domain works (LE allows 5 dupes per week) but isn't elegant — share /var/lib/caddy/certificates between hosts via rsync if round-robin path chosen.
proposed_resolution: Floating IP recommended. Trade-off: round-robin needs state replication (chat.db, VAPID keys, .codex/auth.json, .env) from parent to child PLUS cert sharing PLUS reconciliation logic for split-brain at cutover. Floating IP needs none of that — one API call replaces the entire mechanism. Cost difference is €0.50/mo.
blast_radius: Picking floating IP: install.sh must allocate a floating IP on first install, save its ID alongside the host, and reattach it on every clone-over. PWA backend, Caddy config, DNS — all unchanged forever after first install. Picking round-robin: install.sh must replicate chat.db + VAPID + .codex + .env from parent to child during clone; Caddy needs shared cert volume; chat.db needs replay/reconciliation strategy.
rollback_plan: Floating IP: detach via hcloud API, delete the IP, point DNS at the surviving host's primary IP — done in two API calls. Round-robin: remove second A record, decommission second server.
related_decision_log: CTO-DECISION-009 (Namecheap API automation provides the DNS-management capability either path needs)
